---
title: "Assignment 3: Air Quality and For Loop"
author: "Alecia Dezzani"
date: "May 13, 2026"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Part 1 – Build the Dataset
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
```


```{r}
#generate data for day and PM 2.5 levels
day <- seq(1,365,1)
pm25 <- rpois(365, 28)
air_start <- data.frame(day, pm25)
```

```{r}
#check data to make sure it's realistic
ggplot(air_start, aes(x=day, y=pm25)) +
  geom_point()
```

```{r}
# generate data for season as a factor, and risk
air_data <- air_start |>
  mutate(season = case_when(
  day >= 271 ~ "fall",
  day >= 181 ~ "summer",
  day >= 91 ~ "spring",
  TRUE        ~ "winter")) |>
  mutate( risk = NA)
```

## Part 2 – Classify Risk with a For Loop

```{r}
for (i in 1:365) {
  air_data$risk[i] = case_when(
   air_data$pm25[i] < 12 ~ "good",
   air_data$pm25[i] > 35.4 ~ "unhealthy",
   air_data$pm25[i] <= 35.4 ~ "moderate",
    TRUE ~ "NA"
  )
}
```
